title: CSS Flexbox and Grid Layout
description: Master modern CSS layout techniques with Flexbox and Grid. Learn to create responsive, flexible layouts that work across all devices and screen sizes.
learningObjectives: Use CSS Grid for two-dimensional layouts
Build complex layouts with modern CSS

subtopics.title: Flexbox Basics
subtopics.content:
Flexbox is a one-dimensional layout method for arranging items in rows or columns.

**Enabling Flexbox:**
```css
.container {
  display: flex;
}
```

**Flex Container Properties:**
```css
.container {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: row; /* row, column, row-reverse, column-reverse */
  flex-wrap: nowrap; /* nowrap, wrap, wrap-reverse */
  justify-content: flex-start; /* flex-start, flex-end, center, space-between, space-around, space-evenly */
  align-items: stretch; /* stretch, flex-start, flex-end, center, baseline */
  align-content: stretch; /* stretch, flex-start, flex-end, center, space-between, space-around */
}
```

**Flex Item Properties:**
```css
.item {
  flex-grow: 0; /* How much to grow */
  flex-shrink: 1; /* How much to shrink */
  flex-basis: auto; /* Initial size */
  flex: 0 1 auto; /* Shorthand */
  align-self: auto; /* Override align-items */
}
```

glossary.definition: A one-dimensional layout method for arranging items in rows or columns.
The parent element with display: flex that contains flex items.
A direct child element of a flex container.
A two-dimensional layout system for working with rows and columns.
Defines the structure of grid columns and rows.
A rectangular area on the grid that can contain one or more grid items.
The space between grid items or flex items.
Grid keyword that automatically fits as many columns as possible.
Grid function that sets minimum and maximum size for grid tracks.
